value: pass=2; blocked=6
interpretation: All current no-go rules are blocked or failing until raw microdata, outcome, climate, and validation gates pass.

If fewer than 6 countries have consumption, OOP health expenditure, usable geography, and survey timing, do not proceed with the main multi-country financial-protection paper. Next action: complete raw downloads and verify consumption, OOP, geography, timing, and weights

If fewer than 10 country-waves support both financial hardship and forgone care, keep UHC double failure secondary. Next action: verify financial and access outcome concepts in raw data before promoting the composite outcome
If geolocation is too weak for most countries, use admin-level climate aggregation and lower causal claims. Next action: inspect raw timing/geography variables and classify GPS/admin linkage quality

If event rates are below 3%, expand outcomes or countries; do not force rare-event ML. Next action: run promoted descriptive diagnostics after climate-linked data before treating event-rate checks as model-ready
If leave-country-out predictive performance is poor, do not claim transportable targeting. Next action: run predictive ML only after audited outcomes and climate-linked data exist
If climate lead placebo predicts outcomes, treat causal design as weak. Next action: estimate main reduced-form model, then run future climate lead placebo

If CATE/policy learning does not beat simple targeting rules out of sample, report null targeting value honestly. Next action: do not run causal ML or policy learning until identification and validation gates pass

If only descriptive evidence survives, write a descriptive data paper and do not claim causal effects. Next action: resolve climate-linkage gates, then run descriptive diagnostics before deciding manuscript scope
